title: Common errors and how to fix them
text: Wrong argument types, unknown datasets, unknown fields: Fix: message lists spec defines; correct call.
code: apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "NOPE") Error: ! `dataset` must be one of the spec's datasets. ✖ "NOPE" is not in the spec. ℹ Available: "TS", "DM", "VS", and "SUPPDM".

title: Common errors and how to fix them
text: Cross-slot integrity construction: variable referencing unknown dataset codelist, duplicate definitions, mixed standards, disagreeing study fields: Fix: repair spec source — add missing slot rows, scope read (read_spec(path, datasets = )) one standard.
code: artoo_spec(   data.frame(dataset = "DM"),   data.frame(dataset = "DM", variable = "SEX", data_type = "string",              codelist_id = "C99999") ) Error: ! Some variables reference a codelist not in `codelists`. ✖ Unresolved codelist_id: "C99999". ℹ Add the codelist's terms to `codelists`.

title: Common errors and how to fix them
text: spec says integer; data carries fractions (overflows R’s 32-bit range). conformance = setting bypasses — package’s core guarantee, condition carries offenders data: Fix: retype spec variable "float" "decimal". Collect every offender programmatically tryCatch(..., artoo_error_type = function(cnd) cnd$variables).
code: vars <- spec_variables(adam_spec) vars$data_type[vars$variable == "AGE"] <- "integer" strict <- artoo_spec(adam_spec@datasets, vars,                      codelists = adam_spec@codelists) raw <- cdisc_adsl raw$AGE[1] <- raw$AGE[1] + 0.5 apply_spec(raw, strict, "ADSL", conformance = "off") Error: ! Coercion to the spec dataTypes would lose data. ✖ Integer coercion would truncate fractional values in: AGE (1). ℹ This gate is separate from `conformance`; `conformance = "off"` does not   bypass it. ℹ To keep these values in R, set `apply_spec(on_coercion_loss = "keep")`, or   retype the spec with `set_type()` (dataType "float" or "decimal"). ℹ To see every finding at once, run `check_spec(x, spec, dataset)`.

title: Common errors and how to fix them
text: decode_column() met values absent codelist maps : Fix: clean source values, pass no_match = "keep" / "na" carrying intended. (codelist problem — destination’s terms line source values — translate two hops; see ?decode_column.)
code: dm <- cdisc_dm dm$SEX[1] <- "X9" decode_column(dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", from = "SEX", to = "SEXDECD") Error: ! Values in `SEX` are not in codelist "CL.SEX". ✖ Unmatched: "X9". ℹ Set `no_match = "keep"` or `"na"` to allow them.

title: Common errors and how to fix them
text: Unencodable values target encoding, invalid UTF-8, corrupt mis-shaped files: Fix: re-read source true encoding = (see artoo_encodings() name ecosystem), choose explicit on_invalid policy writer.
code: dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): `BRTHDTC`. dm$USUBJID[1] <- rawToChar(as.raw(c(0x63, 0xE9))) write_json(dm, tempfile(fileext = ".json")) Error in `write_json()`: ! Cannot encode 1 value as UTF-8. ✖ Invalid bytes (hex-escaped): "c<e9>". ℹ Re-read the source with the correct `encoding`, or set `on_invalid`.

what: artoo_error_conformance — the data does not match the spec
title: Common errors and how to fix them
text: apply_spec(conformance = "abort") met error-severity findings: Fix: complete report rides condition (tryCatch(..., artoo_error_conformance = function(cnd) cnd$findings)); fix data (spec) finding finding. default conformance = "warn" report attached returned frame — read conformance(x).

what: 1. The core guarantee: lossless or loud
title: Validation & qualification
text: artoo’s design rule operation silently damages data. shows three enforced behaviors: Lossless round-trips. Every format carries complete CDISC-shaped metadata, read-write-read returns values metadata: Lossy operations abort. coercion truncate overflow aborts (artoo_error_type) touching value; unencodable character aborts (artoo_error_codec) unless opt named policy. silent-truncation path package. title: Conform a data frame to its spec — apply_spec
text: Ordered pipeline. Four fixed steps run order: coerce column CDISC dataType, reorder columns spec, sort rows dataset keys, stamp metadata. spec variable data lacks never fabricated empty column: artoo lossless carrier, deriver. reported instead, informational heads-apply time plus missing_variable finding (mandatory) missing_permissible (), left absent, conformed frame carries columns data actually . Extras kept default. column spec declare survives pipeline (ordered declared ones), reported extra_variable conformance finding, round-trips every write_*() codec metadata inferred R class — membership reported, never enforced silent destruction. Keeping default artoo lossless construction: metadata-application step silently discarded columns break contract, trimming data always explicit, announced choice rather default side effect. extra = "drop" opts trim--spec (returned frame carries exactly spec's columns): undeclared columns removed check, findings describe exactly returned frame (dropped column never reported extra_variable), drop always announced (artoo_message_apply) audit trail removed — even conformance = "". Lossless abort, call. coercion damage values — integer dataType truncating fractions overflowing R's 32-bit range — aborts artoo_error_type value touched, default on_coercion_loss = "error". gate independent conformance: conformance = "" bypass . data (spec) right, set on_coercion_loss = "keep": column keeps wider source type divergence reported integer_fraction / integer_overflow finding, never silently truncated. spec wrong, retype set_type() (repair_spec() findings). error abort carries offending rows data: cnd$variables data frame columns variable, data_type, n, reason ("truncated" / "overflowed"), pipeline can collect every mismatch one tryCatch(..., artoo_error_type = function(cnd) cnd$variables) pass. NA-introduction warning (artoo_warning_coercion) carries frame reason = "na_introduced", conformance = "abort" failure carries complete findings frame cnd$findings. Values never translated. Coded variables keep submission values (SEX stays "M"); codelist translation verb, decode_column().

code: # ---- Example 1: conform ADSL, then read its metadata ---- # # The bundled adam_spec describes ADSL; the raw frame is coerced, # ordered, sorted, and stamped with the CDISC metadata get_meta() reads # back. Variables the spec declares but this extract never derived are # reported (not added), readable via conformance(). adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL") #> 6 variables the spec declares are absent from the data (not added): #> `TRTDURD`, `DISONDT`, `EOSSTT`, `DCSREAS`, `EOSDISP`, and `MMS1TSBL`. #> ℹ See `conformance(x)` for the findings. get_meta(adsl)@dataset$records #> [1] 60  # ---- Example 2: extras are kept and reported, or dropped on request ---- # # By default a column outside the spec rides along (reported by the # extra_variable finding) and still writes losslessly; extra = "drop" # trims to the spec, announced and still reported. DM is SDTM, so it # conforms against the bundled sdtm_spec. raw <- cdisc_dm raw$DERIVED <- seq_len(nrow(raw)) dm <- apply_spec(raw, sdtm_spec, "DM") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. #> ℹ See `conformance(x)` for the findings. findings <- conformance(dm) findings[findings$check == "extra_variable", c("variable", "message")] #>    variable                                        message #> 2  RFXSTDTC Column 'RFXSTDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 3  RFXENDTC Column 'RFXENDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 4   RFICDTC  Column 'RFICDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 5  RFPENDTC Column 'RFPENDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 6    DTHDTC   Column 'DTHDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 7     DTHFL    Column 'DTHFL' is not declared in the spec. #> 8  ACTARMCD Column 'ACTARMCD' is not declared in the spec. #> 9    ACTARM   Column 'ACTARM' is not declared in the spec. #> 10    DMDTC    Column 'DMDTC' is not declared in the spec. #> 11     DMDY     Column 'DMDY' is not declared in the spec. #> 12  DERIVED  Column 'DERIVED' is not declared in the spec. trimmed <- apply_spec(raw, sdtm_spec, "DM", extra = "drop") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. #> ℹ See `conformance(x)` for the findings. #> Dropped 11 undeclared variables: `RFXSTDTC`, `RFXENDTC`, `RFICDTC`, #> `RFPENDTC`, `DTHDTC`, `DTHFL`, `ACTARMCD`, `ACTARM`, `DMDTC`, `DMDY`, #> and `DERIVED` "DERIVED" %in% names(trimmed) #> [1] FALSE

title: Control which conformance checks run — artoo_checks
text: missing_variable Flag mandatory spec variables absent data. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. missing_permissible Flag permissible (non-mandatory) spec variables absent data. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. extra_variable Flag data columns spec declare. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. type_mismatch Flag columns whose storage differs spec dataType. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. length_overflow Flag character values longer spec length. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. char_length_limit Flag character values longer SAS XPORT v5 / FDA 200-byte limit. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist_membership Flag values outside closed codelist. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist_membership_extensible Flag values outside extensible codelist's enumerated terms. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. codelist whose extended flag TRUE allows sponsor terms, non-member note, never error; toggle silences notes independently codelist_membership. label_match Flag column whose label attribute differs spec label. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. key_uniqueness Flag dataset whose spec key variables uniquely identify rows. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. display_format Flag date/datetime/time variable whose displayFormat recognized SAS format family. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. variable_name Flag data column name violates XPORT naming rules. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. 8 characters (v5 limit), 32 (v8 limit), containing anything ASCII letters, digits, underscore. dataset_name Flag dataset name violates XPORT naming rules. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. limits variable_name. label_length Flag column label attribute 40-byte XPORT v5 / FDA limit. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. integer_overflow Flag integer-typed variable holding values beyond R's 32-bit integer range. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. values become NA coercion, error, warning. integer_fraction Flag integer-typed variable holding fractional values. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. Coercion truncate (162.6 becomes 162) — data-integrity event; fix spec dataType (float / decimal) data conforming. iso8601_format Flag character date/datetime/time variable whose values valid ISO 8601 text. <logical(1)>: default TRUE. character column temporal dataType CDISC --DTC form; complete values, right-truncated partials ("1951", "1951-12"), SDTMIG hyphen placeholders ("2003---15") pass, "12NOV2019" impossible calendar date flagged.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: datasets Dataset-level metadata table. <data.frame>: required. One row per dataset; must carry dataset column. Optional columns label, class, structure, keys filled NA absent. variables Variable-level metadata table. <data.frame>: required. One row per variable; must carry dataset, variable, data_type. data_type column canonicalised CDISC dataType (e.g. "text" becomes "string"). Requirement: every dataset value must appear datasets. codelists Controlled-terminology terms. <data.frame> | NULL. Must carry codelist_id term supplied. Interaction: every codelist_id referenced variables must resolve . study Study-level metadata. <data.frame> | NULL. single row named study fields. Well-known fields canonicalised study_name, study_description, protocol_name (aliases StudyName studyid resolve automatically); fields pass verbatim. standard field, present, consumed @standard. values Value-level (VLM) metadata. <data.frame> | NULL. methods Derivation methods. <data.frame> | NULL. Define-XML method definitions variables reference method_id; must carry method_id supplied. Completeness (e.g. referenced method description) checked validate_spec(), . comments Comment definitions. <data.frame> | NULL. Referenced comment_id; must carry comment_id supplied. documents Document references. <data.frame> | NULL. Referenced document_id; must carry document_id supplied. standard CDISC standard spec implements. <character(1)> | NULL. E.g. "ADaMIG 1.1" "SDTMIG 3.2". NULL (default) resolved datasets$standard study$standard; absent everywhere, @standard NA. Restriction: sources must agree one value; conflicting standards abort artoo_error_spec.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: validated artoo_spec object. Inspect spec_datasets() / spec_variables(), check validate_spec().

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
text: Coerce, validate. table first coerced plain data frame (tibble accepted demoted); known columns cast storage mode absent optional columns added typed NA, every downstream reader can trust schema. Validation runs coercion, completed slots. Type canonicalisation. variables$data_type mapped closed CDISC dataType vocabulary (string, integer, decimal, float, double, boolean, date, datetime, time, URI). Common SAS / P21 spellings resolve automatically ("text", "Char", "integer (8)", ...); unrecognised token aborts artoo_error_type. Cross-slot integrity. Construction fails (artoo_error_spec) variable names dataset absent datasets, references codelist_id absent codelists. One spec, one standard. artoo_spec carries exactly one CDISC standard, stored scalar @standard property. constructor resolves standard argument, standard column datasets (P21 workbook shape), standard field study (Define-XML shape) — columns consumed, @standard single home. one distinct value aborts artoo_error_spec; scope source one standard (e.g. read_spec(path, datasets = ...)) instead mixing. One study vocabulary. Well-known study fields canonicalised CDISC ODM GlobalVariables names, snake_cased: study_name, study_description, protocol_name. Source spellings resolve automatically (StudyName, studyid, ...); fields vocabulary know pass verbatim. Aliases disagree value abort artoo_error_spec.

title: Construct a CDISC specification — artoo_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: build a spec from the bundled CDISC-pilot tables ---- # # `cdisc_sdtm_datasets` and `cdisc_sdtm_variables` hold the CDISC pilot SDTM # metadata in the shape artoo_spec() expects; the constructor # canonicalises every type and checks cross-slot integrity. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_sdtm_datasets, cdisc_sdtm_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) spec_datasets(spec) #> [1] "DM"  # ---- Example 2: a focused spec for a single dataset ---- # # Slice the bundled tables to one dataset (DM) to build a smaller spec. dm_ds <- cdisc_sdtm_datasets[cdisc_sdtm_datasets$dataset == "DM", ] dm_var <- cdisc_sdtm_variables[cdisc_sdtm_variables$dataset == "DM", ] dm_spec <- artoo_spec(dm_ds, dm_var, codelists = cdisc_codelists) head(spec_variables(dm_spec, "DM")[, c("variable", "label", "data_type")]) #>   variable                             label data_type #> 1  STUDYID                  Study Identifier    string #> 2   DOMAIN               Domain Abbreviation    string #> 3  USUBJID         Unique Subject Identifier    string #> 4   SUBJID  Subject Identifier for the Study    string #> 5  RFSTDTC Subject Reference Start Date/Time    string #> 6  RFENDTC   Subject Reference End Date/Time    string

Typically output apply_spec(), frame works. spec specification check . <artoo_spec>: required. dataset dataset whose rules apply. <character(1)>: required. Restriction: must name dataset spec (see spec_datasets()). decode codelist column membership checked . <character(1)>. title: Check a dataset against its spec — check_spec
text: Findings, enforcement. check_spec() never modifies data; returns every divergence finds. apply_spec() runs decides via conformance argument (warn, abort, ). dimensions checked : missing variables (split mandatory, error, permissible, warning), extra variables (data column spec declare), type mismatch, ISO 8601 validity character date/datetime/time values (CDISC partials pass; "12NOV2019" ), fractional values 32-bit overflow integer dataType (corrupt data coercion), character length overflow, hard 200-byte XPORT v5 / FDA character limit, codelist membership, label drift spec, key uniqueness, displayFormat validity. Decode-aware membership. decode selects codelist column data checked , matching apply_spec()'s decode step: "none"/"to_code" check codelist terms, "to_decode" decodes. apply_spec() threads decode , decoded column wrongly flagged. Fatal vs informational coercion checks. integer_fraction integer_overflow carry error severity: mark data integer dataType hold without loss, apply_spec() refuses coerce (on_coercion_loss governs gate). type_mismatch note: column stored widely spec declares (integer-valued double, instance) coerces cleanly, informational, blocker.

title: Check a dataset against its spec — check_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: a conformed frame surfaces only the genuine gaps ---- # # apply_spec() coerces and orders to spec but never fabricates a variable # the data lacks; checking the result reports the permissible variables # this extract never derived (here, six) instead of hiding them as empty # columns. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") #> 6 variables the spec declares are absent from the data (not added): #> `TRTDURD`, `DISONDT`, `EOSSTT`, `DCSREAS`, `EOSDISP`, and `MMS1TSBL`. nrow(check_spec(adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL")) #> [1] 12  # ---- Example 2: raw data surfaces divergences ---- # # Checking a raw frame with an undeclared column flags the extras. raw <- cdisc_adsl raw$NOTASPEC <- 1 head(check_spec(raw, adam_spec, "ADSL")[, c("check", "variable", "severity")]) #>                 check variable severity #> 1 missing_permissible  TRTDURD  warning #> 2 missing_permissible  DISONDT  warning #> 3 missing_permissible   EOSSTT  warning #> 4 missing_permissible  DCSREAS  warning #> 5 missing_permissible  EOSDISP  warning #> 6 missing_permissible MMS1TSBL  warning

title: Check a whole study against its spec — check_study
code: # ---- Example 1: scan a whole study in one pass ---- # # Loop the conformance check over every dataset's data. A fractional AGE # (the spec types it integer) surfaces as an integer_fraction finding; the # print is a dataset-by-check count matrix. adsl <- cdisc_adsl adsl$AGE <- adsl$AGE + 0.5 check_study(adam_spec, list(ADSL = adsl, ADAE = cdisc_adae)) #> <artoo_study_findings> 2 datasets: 1 error, 11 warnings, 39 notes #>  #>      codelist_membership_extensible extra_variable integer_fraction #> ADAE                              0              0                0 #> ADSL                              1              5                1 #>      label_match missing_permissible type_mismatch #> ADAE           7                   0            17 #> ADSL           3                   6            11 #>  #> i Treat this as a findings frame: filter by severity, or pass it to repair_spec().  # ---- Example 2: feed the findings straight into repair_spec() ---- # # The result is an ordinary findings frame, so repair_spec() consumes it to # flip every integer_fraction / integer_overflow variable across the study. findings <- check_study(adam_spec, list(ADSL = adsl)) fixed <- repair_spec(adam_spec, findings) spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL")$data_type[   spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL")$variable == "AGE" ] #> [1] "float"

One : "to_decode" (default) map codes decoded values ("M" becomes "Male"). title: Derive or translate a variable through its codelist — decode_column
text: codelist applies. codelist attached spec wins (natural direction RACEN-style derivations, numeric variable owns code/decode pairs); declares none, 's codelist used. neither variable references codelist call aborts — nothing map . Mismatched surfaces chain. single call maps ONE codelist, winning codelist's terms (decodes) must line values — CDISC *N convention guarantees RACEN-style pairs, whose decodes character variable's submission values. two codelists share value surface (say SEXN's decodes "Female"/"Male" SEX holds "F"/"M"), unmatched values hit no_match policy; translate two hops instead — decode 's codelist first, to_code destination's:   Soft matches reported, never silent. Values match trimming whitespace (case-folding, ignore_case = TRUE) still map, artoo_warning_codelist naming variants — check_spec() always compares exactly, clean source submission.
code: dm |>   decode_column(spec, "DM", from = "SEX", to = "SEXDECD") |>   decode_column(spec, "DM", from = "SEXDECD", to = "SEXN",                 direction = "to_code")

title: Derive or translate a variable through its codelist — decode_column
code: spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_sdtm_datasets, cdisc_sdtm_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists)  # ---- Example 1: decode a coded variable into a display column ---- # # SEX is coded against C66731; map the codes to their decodes in a new # column, leaving the submission values untouched. dm <- decode_column(cdisc_dm, spec, "DM", from = "SEX", to = "SEXDECD") table(dm$SEX, dm$SEXDECD) #>     #>     Female Male #>   F     29    0 #>   M      0   31  # ---- Example 2: the RACEN pattern, a coded numeric from its decode ---- # # Declare SEXN as an integer variable owning a numeric codelist, then # derive it from SEX's decoded values: to_code maps each decode to its # submission code, and the spec dataType makes the result integer. vars <- rbind(   cdisc_sdtm_variables,   data.frame(     dataset = "DM", variable = "SEXN", label = "Sex (N)",     data_type = "integer", length = 8L, order = NA_integer_,     codelist_id = "SEXN"   ) ) cls <- rbind(   cdisc_codelists,   data.frame(     codelist_id = "SEXN", term = c("1", "2"),     decode = c("F", "M"), order = 1:2   ) ) spec_n <- artoo_spec(cdisc_sdtm_datasets, vars, codelists = cls) dm_n <- decode_column(cdisc_dm, spec_n, "DM",   from = "SEX", to = "SEXN", direction = "to_code" ) str(dm_n$SEXN) #>  int [1:60] 1 2 2 2 1 1 1 2 1 2 ... #>  - attr(*, "label")= chr "Sex (N)"

title: Read the metadata a dataset carries — get_meta
text: <artoo_meta> two properties. @dataset named list dataset-level attributes: itemGroupOID, name, label, records, studyOID, metaDataVersionOID, encoding, keys. @columns named list one entry per variable, carrying itemOID, name, label, dataType, targetDataType, length, displayFormat, informat, keySequence, codelist, significantDigits, origin (absent values NULL). Pass set_meta() re-attach, index directly (meta@columns$AGE$label).

title: Read the metadata a dataset carries — get_meta
code: # ---- Example 1: read metadata off a conformed dataset ---- # # apply_spec() stamps the metadata; get_meta() reads it back as the S7 # object whose @columns holds one CDISC attribute set per variable. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL") meta <- get_meta(adsl) meta@columns$STUDYID #> $itemOID #> [1] "IT.ADSL.STUDYID" #>  #> $name #> [1] "STUDYID" #>  #> $label #> [1] "Study Identifier" #>  #> $dataType #> [1] "string" #>  #> $length #> [1] 12 #>   # ---- Example 2: round-trip metadata across two frames ---- # # The metadata is a portable object: read it off one frame and stamp it # onto another with set_meta(). bare <- as.data.frame(adsl) attr(bare, "metadata_json") <- NULL restamped <- set_meta(bare, meta) identical(get_meta(restamped)@columns, meta@columns) #> [1] TRUE

title: Read a specification from JSON, Excel, or Define-XML — read_spec
text: Three formats, one validator. .json file read artoo native JSON; .xlsx / .xls file read P21 workbook; .xml file read Define-XML 2.x. Either way result built artoo_spec(), type canonicalisation cross-slot integrity checks identical regardless source. Define-XML ingestion (needs xml2 package). ItemGroupDefs become datasets (keys derived ItemRef KeySequence), ItemRef + ItemDef pairs become variables, CodeLists become codelists (def:ExtendedValue = "Yes" marks extended term), MethodDefs / CommentDefs / leaves become supporting slots, ValueListDefs land value-level slot -clauses rendered readable text. Note: ExternalCodeList (MedDRA, ISO-3166) names dictionary, enumerable membership list; dropped, variables referenced carry codelist. Define-XML v1.0 (2005 model) refused guidance. P21 ingestion. Sheets located tolerant alias match (case-, space-, spelling-variant insensitive). Datasets Variables required; Codelists ValueLevel optional (latter becomes spec's value-level slot). Every cell read text, dataset codelist foreign keys forward-filled recover merged cells (Excel reader returns NA continuation rows). key resolved aborts artoo_error_spec rather silently dropped.

title: Read a specification from JSON, Excel, or Define-XML — read_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: round-trip a spec through native JSON ---- # # write_spec() and read_spec() are inverses on the JSON path: the spec # that comes back is identical to the one written. spec <- artoo_spec(cdisc_sdtm_datasets, cdisc_sdtm_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_spec(spec, path) back <- read_spec(path) identical(back, spec) #> [1] TRUE  # ---- Example 2: scope the read to one dataset ---- # # `datasets =` reads just the domain you are working on — validation is # scoped with it, so a problem elsewhere in the workbook cannot block # this dataset. dm_spec <- read_spec(path, datasets = "DM") spec_datasets(dm_spec) #> [1] "DM" head(spec_variables(dm_spec, "DM")[, c("variable", "label", "data_type")]) #>   variable                             label data_type #> 1  STUDYID                  Study Identifier    string #> 2   DOMAIN               Domain Abbreviation    string #> 3  USUBJID         Unique Subject Identifier    string #> 4   SUBJID  Subject Identifier for the Study    string #> 5  RFSTDTC Subject Reference Start Date/Time    string #> 6  RFENDTC   Subject Reference End Date/Time    string

title: Repair a spec from its conformance findings — repair_spec
code: # ---- Example 1: auto-repair an integer/fractional mismatch ---- # # adam_spec types ADSL.AGE as integer. Give it fractional ages and # check_spec() raises an integer_fraction error; repair_spec() flips AGE # (and only AGE) to float, and the corrected spec then applies cleanly. dat <- cdisc_adsl dat$AGE <- dat$AGE + 0.5 findings <- check_spec(dat, adam_spec, "ADSL") fixed <- repair_spec(adam_spec, findings) spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL")$data_type[   spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL")$variable == "AGE" ] #> [1] "float"  # ---- Example 2: nothing to repair is a no-op ---- # # The bundled data conforms, so its findings carry no integer_fraction or # integer_overflow rows and the spec is returned unchanged. clean <- check_spec(cdisc_adsl, adam_spec, "ADSL") identical(repair_spec(adam_spec, clean), adam_spec) #> No "integer_fraction" or "integer_overflow" findings to repair. #> ℹ The spec is returned unchanged. #> [1] TRUE

title: Override a variable's dataType in a spec — set_type
code: # ---- Example 1: retype one variable the data disagrees with ---- # # The bundled adam_spec types ADSL.AGE as integer. If an extract stored it # with fractional values, retype it to float so apply_spec() coerces # without loss. set_type() returns a new spec; the original is untouched. fixed <- set_type(adam_spec, "ADSL", AGE = "float") v <- spec_variables(fixed, "ADSL") v[v$variable == "AGE", c("variable", "data_type")] #>    variable data_type #> 16      AGE     float  # ---- Example 2: retype several at once, original left intact ---- # # Pass any number of variable = type pairs; canonical dataTypes and common # spellings both resolve. The source spec is immutable, so adam_spec still # reports AGE as its original type. patched <- set_type(adam_spec, "ADSL", AGE = "decimal", TRTSDT = "date") spec_variables(adam_spec, "ADSL")$data_type[   spec_variables(adam_spec, "ADSL")$variable == "AGE" ] #> [1] "integer"

The metadata narrows to the kept columns and the new row count. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") meta <- get_meta(adsl) elderly <- adsl[adsl$AGE > 65, c("STUDYID", "USUBJID", "AGE")] synced <- sync_meta(elderly, meta) get_meta(synced)@dataset$records #> [1] 46  # ---- Example 2: a derived column gains a synthesized entry ---- # # A new column the metadata does not describe is profiled from its class, # so the frame still writes losslessly. adsl$AGEGR9 <- ifelse(adsl$AGE > 65, ">65", "<=65") synced2 <- sync_meta(adsl) #> Synthesized metadata for 1 new column: `AGEGR9`. #> ℹ Edit the spec (or the meta) if the inferred types need refining. get_meta(synced2)@columns$AGEGR9$dataType #> [1] "string"

code: # ---- Example 1: validate one dataset ---- # # Build a spec from the bundled ADaM tables and validate it; the # result prints a sectioned report and keeps the findings table. spec <- artoo_spec(   cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables,   codelists = cdisc_codelists ) chk <- validate_spec(spec, dataset = "ADSL") chk@findings #>                check dimension severity dataset variable #> 1 study_name_present     study  warning    <NA>     <NA> #>                                               message #> 1 No study-level metadata; the study name is unknown.  # ---- Example 2: gate on errors with on_error = "abort" ---- # # Point a key at a missing variable, then validate with on_error = "abort" # and catch the resulting error. bad_ds <- cdisc_sdtm_datasets bad_ds$keys <- "NOTAVAR" bad <- artoo_spec(bad_ds, cdisc_sdtm_variables, codelists = cdisc_codelists) tryCatch(   validate_spec(bad, dataset = "DM", on_error = "abort"),   artoo_error_validation = function(e) conditionMessage(e)[1] ) #> [1] "\033[1m\033[22mSpec is not submission-ready, 1 error-severity finding.\n\033[31m✖\033[39m Dataset 'DM' keys reference variables not in the spec: NOTAVAR.\n\033[36mℹ\033[39m Inspect every finding in the returned artoo_check."

title: Write a dataset to CDISC Dataset-JSON — write_json
text: Full metadata, loss. Unlike .xpt, .json file records complete artoo_meta: keySequence, codelist, origin, targetDataType, significantDigits survive. Dates, datetimes, times exchanged ISO 8601 strings, SAS-epoch numbers targetDataType "integer" (ADaM numeric-date convention); decimal rides string exact precision preserved. file always UTF-8 (RFC 8259 / CDISC v1.1). NaN infinite values valid CDISC numerics abort write. the columns() pane on # the written file shows the full metadata the file carries (DM is SDTM, # so it conforms against the bundled sdtm_spec). dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. path2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_json(dm, path2, created = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", tz = "UTC")) columns(path2) #> <artoo_columns> DM -- 25 variables, 60 obs #> #   Variable  Type  Len  Format  Label                              Key #> 1   STUDYID   Char  7            Study Identifier                   1 #> 2   DOMAIN    Char  2            Domain Abbreviation #> 3   USUBJID   Char  14           Unique Subject Identifier          2 #> 4   SUBJID    Char  6            Subject Identifier for the Study #> 5   RFSTDTC   Char  10           Subject Reference Start Date/Time #> 6   RFENDTC   Char  10           Subject Reference End Date/Time #> 7   SITEID    Char  3            Study Site Identifier #> 8   AGE       Num                Age #> 9   AGEU      Char  5            Age Units #> 10  SEX       Char  16           Sex #> 11  RACE      Char  41           Race #> 12  ETHNIC    Char  22           Ethnicity #> 13  ARMCD     Char  8            Planned Arm Code #> 14  ARM       Char  20           Description of Planned Arm #> 15  COUNTRY   Char  3            Country #> 16  RFXSTDTC  Char  10 #> 17  RFXENDTC  Char  10 #> 18  RFICDTC   Char  1 #> 19  RFPENDTC  Char  16 #> 20  DTHDTC    Char  10 #> 21  DTHFL     Char  1 #> 22  ACTARMCD  Char  8 #> 23  ACTARM    Char  20 #> 24  DMDTC     Char  10 #> 25  DMDY      Num

title: Write a specification to native JSON or a P21 Excel workbook — write_spec
text: Native JSON lossless format. slot written array row objects, NA encoded JSON null numbers full precision, read_spec() rebuilds identical artoo_spec artoo_spec(). Object keys emitted fixed order, writing spec twice yields byte-identical output. P21 xlsx interchange format. Sheets emitted headers P21 reader recognises (Define, Datasets, Variables, ValueLevel, Codelists, Methods, Comments, Documents; empty optional sheets omitted), foreign keys repeated every row (merged cells), spec's spec_standard() Datasets sheet's Standard column. study row writes back Define sheet's Attribute/Value pairs (StudyName, StudyDescription, ProtocolName). Data Type column written Define-XML / ODM vocabulary workbook expects: character variable text (Dataset-JSON string), decimal / double collapse float, boolean / URI text. Columns P21 vocabulary model lost: foreign column carried slot re-emitted verbatim header, xlsx round-trip keeps user columns. Note: fields P21 column (itemoid, target_data_type, per-variable key_sequence) survive xlsx round-trip; persist JSON need spec back exactly. Data Type re-encoding also non-injective: decimal, double, boolean, URI fold float text read-back. Define-XML partialDate / partialDatetime (partial / incomplete subtypes) read base date / datetime – CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1 partial dataType – written back base type.

CDISC metadata beyond (keySequence, codelist, origin, targetDataType, ...) source encoding representable bytes; ride -session artoo_meta sidecar self-describing formats (Dataset-JSON, Parquet, rds). XPORT also distinguish empty string NA (store blanks) drops trailing spaces. Character ISO dates (--DTC) write text. character column whose dataType date/datetime/time numeric targetDataType CDISC ISO 8601 text form — SDTM --DTC convention — stores character variable, partial dates ("1951", "1951-12") included, byte byte. SAS-numeric encoding (DATE9.-style formats) used columns R Date/POSIXct/hms whose metadata records targetDataType = "integer" (ADaM numeric-date convention). character column targetDataType = "integer" aborts loudly — partial date can never become SAS numeric silently.

title: Write a dataset to SAS XPORT — write_xpt
code: spec <- artoo_spec(   cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables,   codelists = cdisc_codelists )  # ---- Example 1: write a conformed dataset as v5 (FDA standard) ---- # # apply_spec() attaches the metadata; write_xpt() carries the label, length, # and SAS format for each variable into the transport file. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") path <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(adsl, path)  # ---- Example 2: v8 for long names, with a frozen timestamp ---- # # Version 8 keeps names over 8 characters; a fixed `created` makes the bytes # reproducible. Reading it back shows the labels, types, and record count # survived the transport. DM is SDTM, so it conforms against the bundled # sdtm_spec. dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. path8 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(dm, path8, version = 8, created = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", tz = "UTC")) get_meta(read_xpt(path8))@dataset$records #> [1] 60

title: List the members of a SAS XPORT transport file — xpt_members
text: v5 recorded row count. v8 member records rows; v5 member's count derived byte span next member (end file) minus trailing padding, -character v5 member whose last row entirely blank reports one row fewer (documented v5 ambiguity, see write_xpt()).

title: List the members of a SAS XPORT transport file — xpt_members
code: spec <- artoo_spec(   cdisc_adam_datasets, cdisc_adam_variables,   codelists = cdisc_codelists )  # ---- Example 1: a single-member file reports one row ---- # # The FDA convention is one dataset per transport file. dm <- apply_spec(cdisc_dm, sdtm_spec, "DM", conformance = "off") #> 1 variable the spec declares is absent from the data (not added): #> `BRTHDTC`. p <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(dm, p) xpt_members(p) #>   member name        label nvars nobs #> 1      1   DM Demographics    25   60  # ---- Example 2: survey a multi-member file, then read one member ---- # # Concatenate two single-member files into one library and list it. adsl <- apply_spec(cdisc_adsl, spec, "ADSL", conformance = "off") p2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") write_xpt(adsl, p2) multi <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") writeBin(   c(     readBin(p, "raw", file.size(p)),     readBin(p2, "raw", file.size(p2))[-(1:240)]   ),   multi ) xpt_members(multi) #>   member name                          label nvars nobs #> 1      1   DM                   Demographics    25   60 #> 2      2 ADSL Subject-Level Analysis Dataset    48   60

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: artoo clean-slate, KISS/DRY redesign package called vport: lightweight, lossless, CDISC-native reader/writer clinical-trial datasets (SAS XPORT, CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1, NDJSON, Apache Parquet, RDS) around one canonical metadata model. Pre-release; backward compatibility kept vport surface.

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: artoo_spec() canonicalises study-level fields CDISC ODM GlobalVariables vocabulary, snake_cased: study_name, study_description, protocol_name. Source spellings resolve automatically (StudyName P21 Define sheet, studyid hand-built specs); unknown fields pass verbatim; aliases disagree abort artoo_error_spec. spec print header, metadata studyOID, validate_spec() read one canonical field, Define-sourced spec longer prints Study: (unspecified) holding study name. Define-XML reader now also extracts StudyDescription, write_spec() writes study row back P21 Define sheet, study metadata survives xlsx round-trip. artoo_spec() single-standard construction: standard promoted per-dataset column scalar @standard property, resolved explicit argument, P21-style datasets$standard column, Define-XML-style study$standard field (columns consumed). Mixing standards aborts artoo_error_spec; scope read read_spec(path, datasets = ) instead. New spec_standard() accessor; spec print header shows standard; native spec JSON carries top-level standard key. artoo_spec() derives per-variable key_sequence dataset’s keys string source provides none (P21 workbook shape), metadata keySequence populated regardless spec source. Every condition artoo raises now carries three-level class chain – artoo_<severity>_<kind>, artoo_<severity>, artoo_condition — tryCatch(artoo_error = ) catches every error kind expect_error(class = "artoo_error") works family-wide. SAS TIME variables now import hms::hms (seconds since midnight); bespoke time class removed. Elapsed times past 24h, negative, fractional-second values round-trip every format; exchange text stays byte-stable. hms joined Imports. time column compared character string now coerces (old class aborted t == "08:30:00"); convert explicitly comparing.

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: apply_spec() gained extra = c("keep", "drop"): "keep" (default) preserves today’s never-drop behavior; "drop" trims output exactly spec’s columns. drop runs conformance check, conformance() result reports columns returned frame keeps (dropped column never phantom extra_variable / variable_name finding). drop never silent: announced (artoo_message_apply) even conformance = "", audit trail; conformance = "abort" error finding (ever spec-declared column) still aborts input never mutated. apply_spec()’s data-protection conditions now carry evidence data, just prose: lossy-coercion abort (artoo_error_type) NA-introduction warning (artoo_warning_coercion) attach cnd$variables — data frame variable, data_type, n, reason — conformance = "abort" failure attaches complete findings frame cnd$findings, pipeline collects every mismatch one tryCatch() pass instead parsing messages. apply_spec() reduced five load-bearing arguments: apply_spec(x, spec, dataset, conformance =, na_position =). pipeline fixed — coerce, order, sort, stamp — subsetting knob (steps, profile dropped); codelist translation lives decode_column() (decode, no_match, trim, ignore_case dropped); checks controls passed check_spec() directly. apply_spec() longer scaffolds: variable spec declares data lacks longer added empty typed-NA column. reported instead, informational heads-apply time plus missing_variable finding (mandatory) missing_permissible (), left absent. artoo lossless carrier, deriver: fabricating empty derived column modified data masked expected variable never produced. apply_spec() never drops column: variable spec declare survives pipeline, reported extra_variable finding, gets class-inferred metadata entry stamp time, round-trips every codec. apply_spec() gained on_coercion_loss = c("error", "keep"), governed gate coercion lose data (integer dataType truncating fractions, overflowing R’s 32-bit range). "error" (default) keeps abort--touching-data behavior; "keep" skips coercion offending column, preserves wider source type, reports divergence integer_fraction / integer_overflow finding instead failing, QC pass can keep every value still see spec disagree. gate independent conformance (conformance = "" never bypassed ), abort now names knob: on_coercion_loss = "keep", set_type(), check_spec(). New check_study(): run check_spec() named list study’s datasets return one stacked findings frame, “whole study submittable?” one call instead per-dataset loop one abort time. result subclasses findings frame (filters like one feeds repair_spec() directly); printing renders dataset--check count matrix. check_spec()’s type_mismatch finding now severity note, warning: column stored widely spec declares (integer-valued double integer dataType, say) coerces cleanly, informational. fatal coercion checks integer_fraction integer_overflow; -ranking type_mismatch unclutters findings report genuine blockers stand .

title: artoo 0.0.0.9000
text: New members(): format-neutral inventory dataset(s) path holds, one row per dataset (file, member, label, records, variables, format), dispatched extension codec registry. SAS XPORT library lists every member, single-dataset file (.json, .ndjson, .parquet, .rds) reports one row, directory inventories dataset file holds. format-neutral companion xpt-specific xpt_members(). New artoo_encodings(): encodings clinical data travels , one row per encoding name ecosystem — sas (session encoding), r (standard IANA name iconv() uses), python (codec) — written SAS programmers never think encodings. Every reader/writer encoding argument accepts sas r spelling. New columns(): SAS PROC CONTENTS / Universal Viewer variable pane (#, Variable, Type, Len, Format, Label, plus CDISC Key sequence, Informat variable carries one), polymorphic stamped frame, plain data frame (attributes inferred), file path dispatched codec registry. pane mirrors physical storage: Char column always shows byte length (inferred spec declares none, ISO-8601 --DTC column never blank), numeric shows none (8-byte IEEE double character width; Define-XML numeric length digit-width kept metadata), format informat names render uppercase. multi-member XPORT path without member = points xpt_members().
